# `cti-stix-validator`

NOTE: This is an [OASIS TC Open Repository](https://www.oasis-open.org/resources/open-repositories/). See the [Governance]
section for more information.

The STIX Validator checks that STIX JSON content conforms to the
requirements specified in the STIX 2.1 specification. In addition to
checking conformance with the [JSON schemas](https://github.com/oasis-open/cti-stix2-json-schemas), the validator checks conformance with
requirements that cannot be specified in JSON schema, as well as with
established "best practices". This validator is non-normative; in
cases of conflict with the STIX 2.1 specification, the specification
takes precedence.

The STIX 2.1 specification contains two types of requirements:
mandatory "MUST" requirements, and recommended "SHOULD" best practice
requirements. The validator checks documents against the "MUST"
requirements using JSON schemas. Some of these mandatory requirements
cannot be implemented in JSON Schema, however, so the validator uses
Python functions to check them. The "SHOULD" requirements are all
checked by Python functions, and options may be used to ignore some or
all of these recommended "best practices."

The only exception to this is the mandatory requirement that an
object's 'type' be one of those defined by a STIX Object in the
specification. This rules out custom objects, so this check was made
optional.

The validator also color-codes its output to make it easier to tell at
a glance whether validation passed.

## `Installation`

The easiest way to install the STIX validator is with pip:

```
$ pip install stix2-validator
```

## `Usage`

### As A Script

The validator comes with a bundled script which you can use to
validate a JSON file containing STIX content:

```
$ stix2_validator <stix_file.json>
```

### As A Library

You can also use this library to integrate STIX validation into your
own tools. You can validate a JSON file:

```python
from stix2validator import validate_file, print_results

results = validate_file("stix_file.json")
print_results(results)
```

You can also validate a JSON string using `validate_string()`, or a Python
dictionary representing a STIX object using `validate_instance()`. For more
information, see the full documentation
[here](https://stix2-validator.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html).

classes.name: stix2validator.v21.errors.JSONError
documentation:
Wrapper for errors thrown by iter_errors() in the jsonschema module.
Makes errors generated by our functions look like those from jsonschema.

functions.name: stix2validator.errors.remove_u
documentation: Remove ugly u'' prefixes from input string

functions.name: stix2validator.errors.pretty_error
documentation:
Return an error message that is easier to read and more useful.
May require updating if the schemas change significantly.

classes.name: stix2validator.errors.PatternError
documentation: Represent a problem with a STIX Pattern.

classes.name: stix2validator.errors.NoJSONFileFoundError
documentation: Represent a problem finding the input JSON file(s).

classes.name: stix2validator.errors.ValidationError
documentation:
Base Exception for all validator-specific exceptions. This can be used
directly as a generic Exception.

classes.name: stix2validator.errors.SchemaInvalidError
documentation: Represent an error with the JSON Schema file itself.

classes.name: stix2validator.errors.SchemaError

methods.name: stix2validator.errors.SchemaError.as_dict
documentation: Returns a dictionary representation.

Represent a JSON Schema validation error.

Args:
    error: An error returned from JSON Schema validation.

Attributes:
    message: The JSON validation error message.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.interop.interop_created_by_ref
documentation: Ensures that all SDOs reference a Producer

modules.name: stix2validator.validator
documentation: Custom jsonschema.IValidator class and validator functions.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator._iter_errors_custom
documentation:
Perform additional validation not possible merely with JSON schemas.

Args:
    instance: The STIX object to be validated.
    checks: A sequence of callables which do the checks.  Each callable
        may be written to accept 1 arg, which is the object to check,
        or 2 args, which are the object and a ValidationOptions instance.
    options: ValidationOptions instance with settings affecting how
        validation should be done.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.is_json
documentation: Returns `True` if the input filename `fn` ends with a JSON extension.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.list_json_files
documentation:
Return a list of file paths for JSON files within `directory`.

Args:

: directory: A path to a directory.
  recursive: If `True`, this function will descend into all

  > subdirectories.

Returns:

: A list of JSON file paths directly under `directory`.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.get_json_files
documentation:
Return a list of files to validate from `files`. If a member of `files`
is a directory, its children with a `.json` extension will be added to
the return value.

Args:

: files: A list of file paths and/or directory paths.
  recursive: If `true`, this will descend into any subdirectories

  > of input directories.

Returns:

: A list of file paths to validate.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.run_validation
documentation:
Validate files based on command line options.

Args:
: options: An instance of `ValidationOptions` containing options for

  : this validation run.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.validate_parsed_json
documentation:
Validate objects from parsed JSON. This supports a single object, or a
list of objects. If a single object is given, a single result is
returned. Otherwise, a list of results is returned.

If an error occurs, a ValidationErrorResults instance or list which
includes one of these instances, is returned.

```
param obj_json

The parsed json

param options

Validation options

return

An ObjectValidationResults instance, or a list of such.
```

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.validate
documentation:
Validate objects from JSON data in a textual stream.

```
param in

A textual stream of JSON data.

param options

Validation options

return

An ObjectValidationResults instance, or a list of such.
```

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.validate_file
documentation:
Validate the input document `fn` according to the options passed in.

If any exceptions are raised during validation, no further validation
will take place.

Args:

: fn: The filename of the JSON file to be validated.
  options: An instance of `ValidationOptions`.

Returns:

: An instance of FileValidationResults.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.validate_string
documentation:
Validate the input `string` according to the options passed in.

If any exceptions are raised during validation, no further validation
will take place.

Args:

: string: The string containing the JSON to be validated.
  options: An instance of `ValidationOptions`.

Returns:

: An ObjectValidationResults instance, or a list of such.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.is_email
documentation: Built-in checker only ensures emails contain an '@'; we want a more robust check

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.from_uri_to_path
documentation:
Convert a URI to a file path if possible.

Note: replace with 'Path.from_uri(schema_path_uri)' when Python 3.13.

Args:
    uri: the URI, potentially containing a file path.

Returns:
    The file path if the URI contains a file path, the URI otherwise.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.patch_schema
documentation:
Patch schemas with two important fixes.

1) _SPECIFICATIONS dictionary inside referencing.jsonschema is a mapping
    between URLs and validators; 'DRAFT201909' and 'DRAFT202012' are now
    only recognized as such if the URLs is https://.

2) For relative references to work, the $id of the schema needs to point
    to a valid URI or path, that can later be used as a key to retrieve
    a loaded resource.

Args:
    schema_data: the schema itself.
    schema_path: the path of the schema (can be both URI or path).

Returns:
    The patched schema.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.retrieve_from_filesystem
documentation:
Callback to retrieve a schema given its path.

Args:
    schema_path_uri: the schema URI.
    schema_dir: the optional directory of local schemas.

Returns:
    A resource loaded with the content.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.load_validator
documentation:
Create a JSON schema validator for the given schema.

Args:
    schema_path: The filename of the JSON schema.
    schema: A Python object representation of the same schema.

Returns:
    An instance of Draft202012Validator.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.find_schema
documentation:
Search the `schema_dir` directory for a schema called `name`.json.
Return the file path of the first match it finds.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.load_schema
documentation:
Load the JSON schema at the given path as a Python object.

Args:
    schema_path: A filename for a JSON schema.

Returns:
    A Python object representation of the schema.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator._get_error_generator
documentation:
Get a generator for validating against the schema for the given object type.

Args:
    name (str): The object type or extension id to find the schema for.
    obj: The object to be validated.
    schema_dir (str): The path in which to search for schemas.
    version (str): The version of the STIX specification to validate
        against. Only used to find base schemas when schema_dir is None.
    default (str): If the schema for the given type cannot be found, use
        the one with this name instead.

Returns:
    A generator for errors found when validating the object against the
    appropriate schema, or None if schema_dir is None and the schema
    cannot be found.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator._get_musts
documentation:
Return the list of 'MUST' validators for the correct version of STIX.

Args:
    options: ValidationOptions instance with validation options for this
        validation run, including the STIX spec version.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator._get_shoulds
documentation:
Return the list of 'SHOULD' validators for the correct version of STIX.

Args:
    options: ValidationOptions instance with validation options for this
        validation run, including the STIX spec version.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator._schema_validate
documentation:
Set up validation of a single STIX object against its type's schema.
This does no actual validation; it just returns generators which must be
iterated to trigger the actual generation.

This function first creates generators for the built-in schemas, then adds
generators for additional schemas from the options, if specified.

Do not call this function directly; use validate_instance() instead, as it
calls this one. This function does not perform any custom checks.

Args:
    obj: STIX object to validate.
    options: ValidationOptions instance with validation options for this
        validation run, including the STIX spec version.
    bundle_version: STIX version of the bundle containing this object, or
        None if the object is not inside a bundle or the bundle has no
        spec_version property.

functions.name: stix2validator.validator.validate_instance
documentation:
Perform STIX JSON Schema validation against STIX input.

Find the correct schema by looking at the 'type' property of the
`instance` JSON object.

Args:
    instance: A Python dictionary representing a STIX object with a
        'type' property.
    options: ValidationOptions instance with validation options for this
        validation run.

Returns:
    A dictionary of validation results

classes.name: stix2validator.validator.BaseResults

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.BaseResults.is_valid
documentation:
Returns `True` if the validation attempt was successful and
`False` otherwise.

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.BaseResults.as_dict
documentation:
Return a dictionary representation of this class.

Keys:

: `'result'`: The validation result. Values will be `True` or
  `False`.

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.BaseResults.as_json
documentation: Returns a JSON representation of this class instance.
Base class for all validation result types.

classes.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults.__init__
documentation:
Initialize this instance.
\:param is_valid: Whether the overall result is valid
\:param filepath: Which file was validated
\:param object_results: Individual object validation results
\:param fatal: A non-validation-related fatal error

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults.object_result
documentation:
Get the object result object, assuming there is only one. Raises
an error if there is more than one.
\:return: The result object
\:raises ValueError: If there is more than one result

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults.object_result
documentation:
Set the object result to a single value. If `object_result` is not a
single value, an error will be raised.
\:param object_result: The result to set
\:raises ValueError: if `object_result` is not a single value.

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults.object_results
documentation:
Get all object results.
\:return: the results

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults.object_results
documentation:
Set the results to an iterable of values. The values will be collected
into a list. A single value is allowed; it will be converted to a
length 1 list.
\:param object_results: The results to set

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.FileValidationResults.log
documentation: Print (log) these file validation results.

Represents validation results for a file.  This entails potentially
several STIX object results, since a file may contain a list of STIX
objects.

classes.name: stix2validator.validator.ObjectValidationResults

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.ObjectValidationResults.errors
documentation: "A list of {class}`SchemaError` validation errors.

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.ObjectValidationResults.as_dict
documentation:
A dictionary representation of the {class}`.ObjectValidationResults`
instance.

Keys:
: - `'result'`: The validation results (`True` or `False`)
  - `'errors'`: A list of validation errors.

Returns:

> A dictionary representation of an instance of this class.

methods.name: stix2validator.validator.ObjectValidationResults.log
documentation: Print (log) these file validation results.

Results of JSON schema validation for a single STIX object.

Args:

: is_valid: The validation result.
  errors: A list of exception strings reported by the JSON validation

  > engine.

  fatal: A fatal error.
  warnings: A list of warning strings reported by our custom validators.
  fn: The filename/path for the file that was validated; None if a string

  > was validated.

Attributes:

: is_valid: `True` if the validation was successful and `False`

  : otherwise.

  object_id: ID of the STIX object.

classes.name: stix2validator.validator.ValidationErrorResults
documentation:
Results of a failed validation due to a raised Exception.

Args:

: error: An `Exception` instance raised by validation code.

Attributes:

: is_valid: Always `False`.
  error: The string representation of the Exception being passed in.
  exception: The exception which produced these results.

modules.name: stix2validator.scripts.stix2_validator
documentation: Validate STIX 2.0 documents against the specification.
!/usr/bin/env python

modules.name: stix2validator.v21.enums
documentation: STIX 2.1 WD04 open vocabularies and other lists

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.enums.media_types
documentation:
Return a list of the IANA Media (MIME) Types.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.enums.char_sets
documentation:
Return a list of the IANA Character Sets.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.enums.protocols
documentation:
Return a list of values from the IANA Service Name and Transport
Protocol Port Number Registry.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.enums.ipfix
documentation:
Return a list of values from the list of IANA IP Flow Information Export
(IPFIX) Entities.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

modules.name: stix2validator.v21.musts
documentation: Mandatory (MUST) requirement checking functions

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.timestamp
documentation: Ensure timestamps contain sane months, days, hours, minutes, seconds.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.get_comparison_string
documentation: Return a string explaining the given comparison operator.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.timestamp_compare
documentation:
Ensure timestamp properties with a comparison requirement are valid.

E.g. `modified` must be later or equal to `created`.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.observable_timestamp_compare
documentation:
Ensure cyber observable timestamp properties with a comparison
requirement are valid.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.object_marking_circular_refs
documentation:
Ensure that marking definitions do not contain circular references (ie.
they do not reference themselves in the `object_marking_refs` property).

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.granular_markings_circular_refs
documentation:
Ensure that marking definitions do not contain circular references (ie.
they do not reference themselves in the `granular_markings` property).

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.marking_selector_syntax
documentation:
Ensure selectors in granular markings refer to items which are actually
present in the object.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.observable_object_references
documentation:
Ensure certain observable object properties reference the correct type
of object.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.artifact_mime_type
documentation:
Ensure the 'mime_type' property of artifact objects comes from the
Template column in the IANA media type registry.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.character_set
documentation:
Ensure certain properties of cyber observable objects come from the IANA
Character Set list.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.language
documentation: Ensure the 'lang' property of SDOs is a valid RFC 5646 language code.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.software_language
documentation:
Ensure the 'language' property of software objects is a valid ISO 639-2
language code.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.patterns
documentation:
Ensure that the syntax of the pattern of an indicator is valid, and that
objects and properties referenced by the pattern are valid.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.cpe_check
documentation: Checks to see if provided cpe is a valid CPE v2.3 entry

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.language_contents
documentation:
Ensure keys in Language Content's 'contents' dictionary are valid
language codes, and that the keys in the sub-dictionaries match the rules
for object property names.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.uuid_version_check
documentation:
Ensure that an SCO with only optional ID Contributing Properties use a
UUIDv4

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.process
documentation: Ensure that process objects use UUIDv4

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.musts.list_musts
documentation: Construct the list of 'MUST' validators to be run by the validator.

modules.name: stix2validator
documentation: Expose certain functions and classes to the stix2validator namespace
flake8: noqa

classes.name: stix2validator.v20.errors.JSONError
documentation:
Wrapper for errors thrown by iter_errors() in the jsonschema module.
Makes errors generated by our functions look like those from jsonschema.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.set_level
documentation:
Set the output level for the application.
If `verbose_output` is False then the application does not print
informational messages to stdout; only results or fatal errors are printed
to stdout.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.set_silent
documentation:
Set the silent flag for the application.
If `silence_output` is True then the application does not print
any messages to stdout.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.error
documentation:
Print a message to the stderr prepended by '[X]' and calls
```sys.exit(status)``.

Args:
    msg: The error message to print.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.info
documentation:
Print a message to stdout, prepended by '[-]'.

Note:
    If the application is not running in verbose mode, this function will
    return immediately and no message will be printed.

Args:
    msg: The message to print.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_level
documentation:
Print a formatted message to stdout prepended by spaces. Useful for
printing hierarchical information, like bullet lists.

Note:

: If the application is running in "Silent Mode"
  (i.e., `_SILENT == True`), this function will return
  immediately and no message will be printed.

Args:

: log_function: The function that will be called to output the formatted

  : message.

  fmt (str): A Python formatted string.
  level (int): Used to determing how many spaces to print. The formula

  > is ' ' * level .

  args: Variable length list of arguments. Values are plugged into the

  : format string.

Examples:
: ```pycon
  >>> print_level("%s %d", 0, "TEST", 0)
  TEST 0
  >>> print_level("%s %d", 1, "TEST", 1)
      TEST 1
  >>> print_level("%s %d", 2, "TEST", 2)
          TEST 2
  ```

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_fatal_results
documentation: Print fatal errors that occurred during validation runs.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_schema_results
documentation:
Print JSON Schema validation errors to stdout.

Args:
    results: An instance of ObjectValidationResults.
    level: The level at which to print the results.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_warning_results
documentation: Print warning messages found during validation.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_horizontal_rule
documentation:
Print a horizontal rule.

Note:

: If the application is running in "Silent Mode"
  (i.e., `_SILENT == True`), this function will return
  immediately and nothing will be printed.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_results_header
documentation: Print a header for the results of either a file or an object.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_object_results
documentation:
Print the results of validating an object.

Args:
    obj_result: An ObjectValidationResults instance.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_file_results
documentation:
Print the results of validating a file.

Args:
    file_result: A FileValidationResults instance.

functions.name: stix2validator.output.print_results
documentation:
Print `results` (the results of validation) to stdout.

Args:
    results: A list of FileValidationResults or ObjectValidationResults
             instances.

functions.name: stix2validator.util.parse_args
documentation:
Parses a list of command line arguments into a ValidationOptions object.

Args:
    cmd_args (list of str): The list of command line arguments to be parsed.
    is_script: Whether the arguments are intended for use in a stand-alone
        script or imported into another tool.

Returns:
    Instance of ``ValidationOptions``

functions.name: stix2validator.util.has_cyber_observable_data
documentation:
Return True if the given instance is an observed-data object
containing cyber observables or is a cyber observable itself.

classes.name: stix2validator.util.NewlinesHelpFormatter
documentation: Custom help formatter to insert newlines between argument help texts.

classes.name: stix2validator.util.ValidationOptions

methods.name: stix2validator.util.ValidationOptions.set_check_codes
documentation: Set which checks are enabled/disabled.

Collection of validation options which can be set via command line or
programmatically in a script.

It can be initialized either by passing in the result of parse_args() from
argparse to the cmd_args parameter, or by specifying individual options
with the other parameters.

Attributes:

: cmd_args: An instance of `argparse.Namespace` containing options

  : supplied on the command line.

  version: The version of the STIX specification to validate against.
  verbose: True if informational notes and more verbose error messages

  > should be printed to stdout/stderr.

  silent: True if all output to stdout should be silenced.
  files: A list of input files and directories of files to be

  > validated.

  recursive: Recursively descend into input directories.
  schema_dir: A user-defined schema directory to validate against.
  disabled: List of "SHOULD" checks that will be skipped.
  enabled: List of "SHOULD" checks that will be performed.
  strict: Specifies that recommended requirements should produce errors

  > instead of mere warnings.

  strict_types: Specifies that no custom object types be used, only

  : those defined in the STIX specification.

  strict_properties: Specifies that no custom properties be used, only

  : those defined in the STIX specification.

  enforce_refs:Ensures that all SDOs being referenced by the SRO are

  : contained within the same bundle

modules.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds
documentation:
Recommended (SHOULD) requirement checking functions

To add a new check:
- in this module:
    - define a new function
    - add the function to CHECKS
    - add the function to list_shoulds()
- in enums.py:
    - add the check code and name to CHECK_CODES
- in util.py:
    - add the check code and name to table
- in docs/best-practices:
    - add the check code and name to table

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_prefix_strict
documentation: Ensure custom content follows strict naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom content follows lenient naming style conventions
for forward-compatibility.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_object_prefix_strict
documentation: Ensure custom objects follow strict naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_object_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom objects follow lenient naming style conventions
for forward-compatibility.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_property_prefix_strict
documentation:
Ensure custom properties follow strict naming style conventions.
Does not check property names in custom objects.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_property_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom properties follow lenient naming style conventions
for forward-compatibility.
Does not check property names in custom objects.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds._uses_extension
documentation:
Return true if instance contains an extension using any of the given
extension_types.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.extensions_use
documentation:
Ensure custom objects, properties, and observable extensions have been
implemented with Extension Definitions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_object
documentation: Ensure custom objects use the extension mechanism.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_property
documentation:
Ensure custom properties are implemented using the 2.1 extension
mechanism.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_embedded_property
documentation:
Ensure custom properties in observable object embedded properties are
implemented using the 2.1 extension mechanism.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_extension_property
documentation:
Ensure custom properties in predefined observable object extensions are
implemented using the 2.1 extension mechanism.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_extension
documentation:
Ensure custom observable object extensions are implemented using the 2.1
extension mechanism.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.deprecated_property_check
documentation: Check to see if any included properties are deprecated within the spec

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.indicator_property_check
documentation: Check to see if name and decription properties are present

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.uuid_check
documentation:
Ensure Domain Objects, Relationship Objects, Meta Objects, and Bundles
use UUIDv4 for their IDs, and Cyber Observables use UUIDv5.

Process objects are an exception; they are recommended to use UUIDv4.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.open_vocab_values
documentation:
Ensure that the values of all properties which use open vocabularies are
in lowercase and use hyphens instead of spaces or underscores as word
separators.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.kill_chain_phase_names
documentation:
Ensure the `kill_chain_name` and `phase_name` properties of
`kill_chain_phase` objects follow naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.check_vocab
documentation:
Ensure that the open vocabulary specified by `vocab` is used properly.

This checks properties of objects specified in the appropriate `_USES`
dictionary to determine which properties SHOULD use the given vocabulary,
then checks that the values in those properties are from the vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.vocab_marking_definition
documentation:
Ensure that the `definition_type` property of `marking-definition`
objects is one of the values in the STIX 2.0 specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.relationships_strict
documentation:
Ensure that only the relationship types defined in the specification are
used.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.valid_hash_value
documentation:
Return true if given value is a valid, recommended hash name according
to the STIX 2 specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.vocab_hash_algo
documentation:
Ensure objects with 'hashes' properties only use values from the
hash-algorithm-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.vocab_windows_pebinary_type
documentation:
Ensure file objects with the windows-pebinary-ext extension have a
'pe-type' property that is from the windows-pebinary-type-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.vocab_account_type
documentation:
Ensure a user-account objects' 'account-type' property is from the
account-type-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.observable_object_keys
documentation: Ensure observable-objects keys are non-negative integers.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.test_dict_keys
documentation:
Recursively generate errors for incorrectly formatted cyber observable
dictionary keys.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.observable_dictionary_keys
documentation:
Ensure dictionaries in the cyber observable layer have lowercase keys
no longer than 30 characters.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_object_prefix_strict
documentation: Ensure custom observable objects follow strict naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_object_prefix_lax
documentation: Ensure custom observable objects follow naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_object_extension_prefix_strict
documentation:
Ensure custom observable object extensions follow strict naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_object_extension_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom observable object extensions follow naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_properties_prefix_strict
documentation:
Ensure observable object custom properties follow strict naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.custom_observable_properties_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure observable object custom properties follow naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.network_traffic_ports
documentation: Ensure network-traffic objects contain both src_port and dst_port.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.mime_type
documentation:
Ensure the 'mime_type' property of file objects comes from the Template
column in the IANA media type registry.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.protocols
documentation:
Ensure the 'protocols' property of network-traffic objects contains only
values from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number
Registry.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.ipfix
documentation:
Ensure the 'ipfix' property of network-traffic objects contains only
values from the IANA IP Flow Information Export (IPFIX) Entities Registry.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.http_request_headers
documentation:
Ensure the keys of the 'request_headers' property of the http-request-
ext extension of network-traffic objects conform to the format for HTTP
request headers. Use a regex because there isn't a definitive source.
https://www.iana.org/assignments/message-headers/message-headers.xhtml does
not differentiate between request and response headers, and leaves out
several common non-standard request fields listed elsewhere.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.socket_options
documentation:
Ensure the keys of the 'options' property of the socket-ext extension of
network-traffic objects are only valid socket options (SO_*).

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.pdf_doc_info
documentation:
Ensure the keys of the 'document_info_dict' property of the pdf-ext
extension of file objects are only valid PDF Document Information
Dictionary Keys.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.countries
documentation:
Ensure that the `country` property of `location` objects is a valid
ISO 3166-1 ALPHA-2 Code.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.windows_process_priority_format
documentation: Ensure the 'priority' property of windows-process-ext ends in '_CLASS'.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.malware_analysis_product
documentation: Ensure product name is all lowercase with words seperated by a dash

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.hash_length
documentation: Ensure keys in 'hashes'-type properties are no more than 30 characters long.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.duplicate_ids
documentation: Ensure objects with duplicate IDs have different `modified` timestamps.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.types_strict
documentation:
Ensure that no custom object types are used, but only the official ones
from the specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.properties_strict
documentation:
Ensure that no custom properties are used, but only the official ones
from the specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.extension_description
documentation: Ensure Extension Definitions have a description.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.extension_properties
documentation:
Ensure toplevel-property-extensions include at least one extension
property.

functions.name: stix2validator.v21.shoulds.list_shoulds
documentation: Construct the list of 'SHOULD' validators to be run by the validator.

modules.name: stix2validator.codes
documentation: Exit status codes

functions.name: stix2validator.codes.get_code
documentation:
Determines the exit status code to be returned from a script by
inspecting the results returned from validating file(s).
Status codes are binary OR'd together, so exit codes can communicate
multiple error conditions.

modules.name: stix2validator.v20.musts
documentation: Mandatory (MUST) requirement checking functions

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.timestamp
documentation: Ensure timestamps contain sane months, days, hours, minutes, seconds.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.modified_created
documentation: `modified` property must be later or equal to `created` property

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.object_marking_circular_refs
documentation:
Ensure that marking definitions do not contain circular references (ie.
they do not reference themselves in the `object_marking_refs` property).

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.granular_markings_circular_refs
documentation:
Ensure that marking definitions do not contain circular references (ie.
they do not reference themselves in the `granular_markings` property).

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.marking_selector_syntax
documentation:
Ensure selectors in granular markings refer to items which are actually
present in the object.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.observable_object_references
documentation:
Ensure certain observable object properties reference the correct type
of object.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.artifact_mime_type
documentation:
Ensure the 'mime_type' property of artifact objects comes from the
Template column in the IANA media type registry.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.character_set
documentation:
Ensure certain properties of cyber observable objects come from the IANA
Character Set list.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.software_language
documentation:
Ensure the 'language' property of software objects is a valid ISO 639-2
language code.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.patterns
documentation:
Ensure that the syntax of the pattern of an indicator is valid, and that
objects and properties referenced by the pattern are valid.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.cpe_check
documentation: Checks to see if provided cpe is a valid CPE v2.3 entry

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.musts.list_musts
documentation: Construct the list of 'MUST' validators to be run by the validator.

modules.name: stix2validator.v20.enums
documentation: STIX 2.0 open vocabularies and other lists

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.enums.media_types
documentation:
Return a list of the IANA Media (MIME) Types.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.enums.char_sets
documentation:
Return a list of the IANA Character Sets.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.enums.protocols
documentation:
Return a list of values from the IANA Service Name and Transport
Protocol Port Number Registry.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.enums.ipfix
documentation:
Return a list of values from the list of IANA IP Flow Information Export
(IPFIX) Entities.
Store it as a function attribute so that we only build the list once.

modules.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds
documentation:
Recommended (SHOULD) requirement checking functions

To add a new check:
- in this module:
    - define a new function
    - add the function to CHECKS
    - add the function to list_shoulds()
- in enums.py:
    - add the check code and name to CHECK_CODES
- in util.py:
    - add the check code and name to table
- in docs/best-practices:
    - add the check code and name to table

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_prefix_lax

for forward-compatibility.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_object_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom objects follow lenient naming style conventions
for forward-compatibility.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_property_prefix_strict
documentation:
Ensure custom properties follow strict naming style conventions.

Does not check property names in custom objects.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_property_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom properties follow lenient naming style conventions
for forward-compatibility.

Does not check property names in custom objects.

documentation:
Ensure that the values of all properties which use open vocabularies are
in lowercase and use hyphens instead of spaces or underscores as word

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.kill_chain_phase_names
documentation:
Ensure the `kill_chain_name` and `phase_name` properties of
`kill_chain_phase` objects follow naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.check_vocab
documentation:
Ensure that the open vocabulary specified by `vocab` is used properly.

This checks properties of objects specified in the appropriate `_USES`
dictionary to determine which properties SHOULD use the given vocabulary,
then checks that the values in those properties are from the vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.vocab_marking_definition
documentation:
Ensure that the `definition_type` property of `marking-definition`
objects is one of the values in the STIX 2.0 specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.relationships_strict
documentation:
Ensure that only the relationship types defined in the specification are

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.valid_hash_value
documentation:
Return true if given value is a valid, recommended hash name according
to the STIX 2 specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.vocab_hash_algo
documentation:
Ensure objects with 'hashes' properties only use values from the
hash-algo-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.vocab_encryption_algo
documentation:
Ensure file objects' 'encryption_algorithm' property is from the
encryption-algo-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.vocab_windows_pebinary_type
documentation:
Ensure file objects with the windows-pebinary-ext extension have a
'pe-type' property that is from the windows-pebinary-type-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.vocab_account_type
documentation:
Ensure a user-account objects' 'account-type' property is from the
account-type-ov vocabulary.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.observable_object_keys
documentation: Ensure observable-objects keys are non-negative integers.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.test_dict_keys
documentation:
Recursively generate errors for incorrectly formatted cyber observable
dictionary keys.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.observable_dictionary_keys
documentation:
Ensure dictionaries in the cyber observable layer have lowercase keys
no longer than 30 characters.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_observable_object_prefix_strict
documentation: Ensure custom observable objects follow strict naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_observable_object_prefix_lax
documentation: Ensure custom observable objects follow naming style conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_object_extension_prefix_strict
documentation:
Ensure custom observable object extensions follow strict naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_object_extension_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure custom observable object extensions follow naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_observable_properties_prefix_strict
documentation:
Ensure observable object custom properties follow strict naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.custom_observable_properties_prefix_lax
documentation:
Ensure observable object custom properties follow naming style
conventions.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.network_traffic_ports
documentation: Ensure network-traffic objects contain both src_port and dst_port.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.mime_type
documentation:
Ensure the 'mime_type' property of file objects comes from the Template

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.protocols
documentation:
Ensure the 'protocols' property of network-traffic objects contains only
values from the IANA Service Name and Transport Protocol Port Number
Registry.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.ipfix
documentation:
Ensure the 'ipfix' property of network-traffic objects contains only

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.http_request_headers
documentation:
Ensure the keys of the 'request_headers' property of the http-request-
ext extension of network-traffic objects conform to the format for HTTP
request headers. Use a regex because there isn't a definitive source.
https://www.iana.org/assignments/message-headers/message-headers.xhtml does
not differentiate between request and response headers, and leaves out
several common non-standard request fields listed elsewhere.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.socket_options
documentation:
Ensure the keys of the 'options' property of the socket-ext extension of
network-traffic objects are only valid socket options (SO_*).

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.pdf_doc_info
documentation:
Ensure the keys of the 'document_info_dict' property of the pdf-ext
extension of file objects are only valid PDF Document Information
Dictionary Keys.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.windows_process_priority_format
documentation: Ensure the 'priority' property of windows-process-ext ends in '_CLASS'.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.hash_length
documentation: Ensure keys in 'hashes'-type properties are no more than 30 characters long.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.enforce_relationship_refs
documentation:
Ensures that all SDOs being referenced by the SRO are contained
within the same bundle

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.duplicate_ids
documentation: Ensure objects with duplicate IDs have different `modified` timestamps.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.types_strict
documentation:
Ensure that no custom object types are used, but only the official ones
from the specification.

functions.name: stix2validator.v20.shoulds.properties_strict
documentation:
Ensure that no custom properties are used, but only the official ones
from the specification.

documentation: Construct the list of 'SHOULD' validators to be run by the validator.
